description: HR & People prompt pack — 6 prompts for HR professionals and people managers

prompts.title: Job Description
prompts.desc: Write a clear, inclusive job description from a role brief.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior HR Business Partner.
[TASK]    Write a job description for the role described below.
[STYLE]   Clear, inclusive, and appealing. Avoid jargon and gendered language.
[FORMAT]  Sections: About the Role, What You'll Do (5-6 bullets), What We're Looking For (5-6 bullets), Nice to Have (3 bullets).
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Candidate Rejection Email
prompts.desc: Write a warm, respectful rejection email to an unsuccessful candidate.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are an HR professional sending a rejection email.
[TASK]    Write a rejection email to the candidate based on the context below.
[STYLE]   Warm, respectful, and specific enough to feel personal. Not a template.
[FORMAT]  3-4 sentences. Thank them, deliver the news clearly, leave the door open.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Performance Improvement Plan
prompts.desc: Draft a structured performance improvement plan from manager notes.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are an HR Business Partner drafting a performance improvement plan.
[TASK]    Write a structured PIP based on the performance issues and context below.
[STYLE]   Professional, clear, and constructive. Focus on behaviours and outcomes, not personality.
[FORMAT]  Sections: Background, Performance Concerns (specific examples), Expected Standards, Support Provided, Timeline and Review Points, Consequences.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Policy Announcement
prompts.desc: Write a company-wide policy announcement that people will actually read.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are an HR professional writing a company-wide policy communication.
[TASK]    Write an announcement for the policy or change described below.
[STYLE]   Clear, human, and direct. Lead with what changes and when, not the backstory.
[FORMAT]  Short intro paragraph, bullet points for key changes, who it affects, effective date, who to contact with questions.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Exit Interview Summary
prompts.desc: Summarise exit interview notes into structured themes and actions.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are an HR Business Partner summarising an exit interview.
[TASK]    Summarise the exit interview notes below into a structured internal report.
[STYLE]   Neutral, factual. No editorialising.
[FORMAT]  Sections: Reason for Leaving, Key Themes (bullets), Manager/Team Feedback, Retention Flags, Recommended Actions.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Offer Letter Email
prompts.desc: Write a warm, professional offer letter email to a selected candidate.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are an HR professional sending a job offer email.
[TASK]    Write a job offer email based on the role and candidate details below.
[STYLE]   Warm and professional. Make the candidate feel genuinely excited.
[FORMAT]  Opening (congratulations), role and start date, key terms summary, next steps, warm close.
[INPUT]
